tasks.subject: Task 1: UI handler + read-only dashboard + smoke tests
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Working read-only dashboard at `GET /` — three cards in a 3-col grid (single col below 900px). Sensors, fans (with sensor-override warning), service info, firmware. Auto-refresh every 5s; stale at 90s. Top-of-page banner on bootstrap-fetch failure.

**Files:**
- Create: `cmd/breezyd/ui.go`
- Create: `cmd/breezyd/ui/index.html`
- Modify: `cmd/breezyd/server.go` (add `GET /{$}` to inner mux)
- Modify: `cmd/breezyd/main.go` (add `mux.Handle("/", handler)` to outer mux)
- Create: `cmd/breezyd/ui_test.go`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- GET / returns 200, text/html, non-empty body containing `<title>breezy</title>`
- GET /v1/devices still returns JSON
- GET /asdf still returns 404
- Cards render with sensor/fan/service/firmware data, refreshing every 5s
- Stale (>90s) cards desaturate, timestamp red
- Sensor-override warning when in_user_control=false
- Bootstrap-fetch failure shows top error banner, retries every 5s

**Verify:** `go test ./cmd/breezyd -run TestUI -v` then manual `curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9876/` and browser load

```json:metadata
{"files": ["cmd/breezyd/ui.go", "cmd/breezyd/ui/index.html", "cmd/breezyd/server.go", "cmd/breezyd/main.go", "cmd/breezyd/ui_test.go"], "verifyCommand": "go test ./cmd/breezyd -run TestUI -v && just check", "acceptanceCriteria": ["GET / returns embedded HTML", "API routes unaffected", "GET /asdf 404s", "cards render with data", "stale indicator at 90s", "sensor-override warning", "error banner on bootstrap failure"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: Per-card controls (power, mode, speed, heater)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Add Controls block per card. Power/Heater toggles, four Mode buttons, Speed segmented control (preset 1/2/3 + manual %), 10..100 step-5 slider that POSTs on `change` (not `input`). Toasts on POST failure; controls disable while in-flight or stale.

**Files:**
- Modify: `cmd/breezyd/ui/index.html` (CSS + control HTML in renderCard + JS click/change handlers + postWrite)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Power/Heater buttons POST {on: <inverse>}
- Mode buttons POST {mode: <name>}
- Preset radios POST {preset: 1|2|3}
- Manual radio enables slider but doesn't POST until slider changes
- Slider fires on change (mouseup/blur), not input
- POST failures show .toast under the failing control with daemon's error text
- Toasts auto-clear on next successful poll
- Controls disabled when in-flight OR card stale
- Controls reflect configured.* not live.*

**Verify:** `just check` (existing tests) + manual click/observe via running daemon

```json:metadata
{"files": ["cmd/breezyd/ui/index.html"], "verifyCommand": "just check", "acceptanceCriteria": ["power POSTs inverse", "mode POSTs name", "preset POSTs preset", "manual radio enables slider only", "slider POSTs on change", "heater POSTs inverse", "toasts on failure", "controls disabled when in-flight or stale"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 3: NixOS module — opt-in nginx reverse-proxy integration
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Add `services.breezyd.nginx.{enable, virtualHost, basicAuthFile}` mirroring the existing `services.breezyd.prometheus` shape. When enabled alongside `services.nginx.enable`, attach a proxy_pass location to a named vhost pointing at the daemon's loopback listener.

**Files:**
- Modify: `nix/module.nix`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Three options: enable (bool, default false), virtualHost (nullOr str, default null), basicAuthFile (nullOr path, default null)
- enable=true without virtualHost OR without services.nginx.enable fails with clear assertion message
- When all three flags align, services.nginx.virtualHosts.<vhost>.locations."/" gets {proxyPass, basicAuthFile}
- Proxy target derives from cfg.settings.daemon.listen with 127.0.0.1:9876 fallback
- openFirewall NOT auto-flipped
- nix-instantiate --parse passes

**Verify:** `nix-instantiate --parse nix/module.nix > /dev/null && echo OK`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["nix/module.nix"], "verifyCommand": "nix-instantiate --parse nix/module.nix > /dev/null && echo OK", "acceptanceCriteria": ["enable/virtualHost/basicAuthFile options", "assertions on misconfig", "proxy_pass injection", "loopback fallback for proxy target", "openFirewall not auto-flipped", "module parses"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 4: Docs — README + CLAUDE.md sections for the UI and nginx integration
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Document the dashboard, both LAN-exposure paths (off-loopback bind, nginx reverse-proxy via the NixOS module), and security implications.

**Files:**
- Modify: `README.md` (Status section, new Web UI section between First run and CLI overview, Security section)
- Modify: `CLAUDE.md` (Architecture section, release-plumbing paragraph)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- README has `## Web UI` section with: bind-address explanation + Behind nginx (NixOS) subsection with full config example
- README Status `In scope` adds dashboard bullet; `Out of scope` removes "No web UI"
- README Security gains paragraph on listener exposure
- CLAUDE.md Architecture mentions ui.go + ui/index.html and `GET /{$}` (load-bearing anchor)
- CLAUDE.md release-plumbing names both prometheus and nginx opt-in module integrations
- No `make` references reintroduced; just check passes

**Verify:** `just check && ! grep -nE 'make (build|test|lint|tidy|clean)' README.md CLAUDE.md && grep -n '## Web UI' README.md && grep -n 'Behind nginx' README.md && grep -n 'GET /{\$}' CLAUDE.md && grep -n 'services.breezyd.nginx' CLAUDE.md`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["README.md", "CLAUDE.md"], "verifyCommand": "just check && ! grep -nE 'make (build|test|lint|tidy|clean)' README.md CLAUDE.md && grep -n '## Web UI' README.md && grep -n 'Behind nginx' README.md", "acceptanceCriteria": ["Web UI section in README", "Behind nginx subsection", "Status section updated", "Security paragraph added", "CLAUDE.md Architecture updated", "CLAUDE.md release-plumbing updated"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 5: Playwright tests for the dashboard
tasks.description:
**Goal:** End-to-end browser tests that intercept all /v1/... calls via page.route() and assert UI's HTTP-call contract (correct method/URL/body) plus UI updates. No real daemon involved — Playwright mocks the routing layer.

**Files:**
- Create: `tests/ui/package.json` (pnpm; @playwright/test only dep)
- Create: `tests/ui/playwright.config.ts`
- Create: `tests/ui/dashboard.spec.ts`
- Modify: `justfile` (add test-ui-install + test-ui recipes using pnpm)
- Modify: `.gitignore` (tests/ui/node_modules, test-results, playwright-report)

**Tooling:** pnpm, NOT npm. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `pnpm exec playwright test`. Do not fall back to npm if pnpm is missing — install pnpm via corepack or Nix.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `just test-ui-install` then `just test-ui` exits 0
- Tests use page.setContent + page.route() — no real breezyd
- Coverage: bootstrap, sensors, stale, sensor override, power, mode (×4), preset, manual slider (one POST on change), heater, error toast on 4xx, daemon-unreachable banner
- pnpm-lock.yaml committed; npm/yarn lockfiles absent
- CI workflow NOT modified (Playwright is opt-in local-dev)

**Verify:** `just test-ui-install && just test-ui`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["tests/ui/package.json", "tests/ui/playwright.config.ts", "tests/ui/dashboard.spec.ts", "tests/ui/pnpm-lock.yaml", "justfile", ".gitignore"], "verifyCommand": "just test-ui", "acceptanceCriteria": ["pnpm install + chromium download", "all 11 specs pass", "page.route mocks all /v1/... calls", "pnpm-lock.yaml committed", "CI unchanged"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 6: First-run config bootstrap
tasks.description:
**Goal:** When breezyd is started against a missing config path, write a sensible default (mode 0600, parent dir 0700) and exit non-zero with a friendly message. Atomic write (temp + rename); refuses to clobber existing files.

Not in the basic-UI spec — orthogonal UX improvement added during plan-writing. Fits on this plan run for execution efficiency.

**Files:**
- Modify: `internal/config/config.go` (add WriteDefault + ErrConfigExists + defaultConfigTemplate)
- Modify: `internal/config/config_test.go` (3 tests: fresh dir, creates parent, refuses to overwrite)
- Modify: `cmd/breezyd/main.go` (gate on errors.Is(_, os.ErrNotExist), call WriteDefault, return friendly error)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- WriteDefault creates parent (0700) if missing, writes file (0600), atomic via temp+rename
- Existing file → ErrConfigExists, no clobber
- Default content has [daemon] block (loopback default + 30s + on-start) + commented-out [devices.playroom] + reference to `breezy discover`
- Running breezyd against a missing path writes default + exits non-zero with stderr containing path + 'edit it'
- Existing-file path unchanged (gated on os.ErrNotExist)
- Tests cover: fresh dir, missing parent, refuses-to-overwrite

**Verify:** `go test ./internal/config -run TestWriteDefault -v && just check && rm -f /tmp/bs.toml && ./breezyd --config /tmp/bs.toml; echo exit=$?; stat -c '%a' /tmp/bs.toml; rm /tmp/bs.toml`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["internal/config/config.go", "internal/config/config_test.go", "cmd/breezyd/main.go"], "verifyCommand": "go test ./internal/config -run TestWriteDefault -v && just check", "acceptanceCriteria": ["WriteDefault atomic write", "refuses to overwrite", "creates parent dir", "main.go gates on os.ErrNotExist", "friendly stderr message", "3 unit tests"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 7: Diagnose why `breezy discover` doesn't find user's units
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Investigate why `breezy discover` returns no/wrong results on the user's LAN. Hypothesis: an mDNS or broadcast-routing issue (working hypothesis only — the protocol uses UDP broadcast on port 4000, not mDNS). Acceptable outcomes: a fix, OR a written-down failure analysis at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-04-discover-investigation.md.

Open-ended investigation task — added during execution-handoff.

**Files (depends on findings):**
- Likely: `pkg/breezy/discover.go`, `pkg/breezy/discover_unix.go`
- Possibly: a new spec doc with the analysis

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `breezy discover` returns all three units (.148, .152, .160) within timeout, OR
- Doc captures: observed failure, packet-capture summary, root-cause hypothesis, suggested fix path, why fix wasn't applied

**Steps:** reproduce → read code → tcpdump capture → triage hypothesis (no outbound / no inbound / decode mismatch / etc.) → fix or document

**Verify:** Manual — `./breezy discover` against the user's host

```json:metadata
{"files": ["pkg/breezy/discover.go", "pkg/breezy/discover_unix.go"], "verifyCommand": "just build && ./breezy discover", "acceptanceCriteria": ["discover finds .148/.152/.160", "OR doc captures the failure analysis"]}
```

tasks.subject: Task 8: Playwright-driven visual verification of the rendered dashboard
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Use Playwright to render the dashboard against a fakedevice-backed breezyd and produce screenshots in 3-col desktop and 1-col mobile viewports. Commit the PNGs so future UI changes show visually in diffs. Add `just screenshot` recipe + `just run-fakedaemon` helper.

Distinct from Task 5: Task 5 mocks the backend and asserts behaviour; Task 8 uses a real backend and produces visual artifacts.

**Files:**
- Create: `tests/ui/screenshot.ts`
- Create: `tests/ui/screenshots/.gitkeep`
- Modify: `tests/ui/package.json` (add tsx + screenshot script)
- Modify: `justfile` (screenshot + run-fakedaemon recipes)
- Commit (binary): `tests/ui/screenshots/dashboard-3col.png`, `dashboard-1col.png`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- `just run-fakedaemon` spins up fakedevice + breezyd on 127.0.0.1:9876
- `just screenshot` produces both PNGs under tests/ui/screenshots/
- Screenshots show 3 cards with sensor/fan/service/control widgets visible; no JS pageerror events
- PNGs committed
- If pageerror fires, STOP — fix the embedded JS before committing screenshots

**Verify:** terminal 1: `just run-fakedaemon`; terminal 2: `just screenshot && file tests/ui/screenshots/*.png` (both should show PNG image data)

```json:metadata
{"files": ["tests/ui/screenshot.ts", "tests/ui/package.json", "tests/ui/pnpm-lock.yaml", "tests/ui/screenshots/.gitkeep", "tests/ui/screenshots/dashboard-3col.png", "tests/ui/screenshots/dashboard-1col.png", "justfile"], "verifyCommand": "just screenshot && file tests/ui/screenshots/*.png", "acceptanceCriteria": ["screenshot.ts renders both viewports", "PNGs committed", "no JS pageerror during render", "run-fakedaemon recipe works"]}
```
